annotation_note: Relations use the graph_creator's allowed verbs where possible: TREATS, CAUSES, PREVENTS, INHIBITS, INTERACTS_WITH, PREDICTS, MEASURED_BY, ASSOCIATED_WITH, REDUCES, INCREASES. Non-standard verbs (e.g. CONTAINS, COMPRISES) are also annotated for the ideal-KG comparison. Strict evaluation requires exact (source, target, type) match; relaxed evaluation matches (source, target) case-insensitively with type ignored.

relationships.evidence: Full XGBoost model AUROC 0.901; biological-feature-only model AUROC 0.898
Logistic regression care-only AUROC 0.660; XGBoost sensitivity analysis 0.729
XGBoost classifiers trained on Sepsis-3 labels using 5-fold stratified cross-validation
Removing care-intensity features reduced AUROC by only 0.003 (AUC drop = 0.0027)
Jaccard similarity = 0.903, indicating high concordance between SIRS-based and SOFA-based definitions
Jaccard similarity = 0.317, indicating major divergence between clinical and administrative definitions
Jaccard similarity = 0.317 at primary site and ~0.20 across multi-center cohorts
MIMIC-IV contains all adult ICU admissions at Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center 2008-2022
AUC drop = 0.076 in eICU multi-center cohort vs 0.003 at BIDMC academic center
Four-phase framework registered on OSF March 11, 2026 before data access
50,000 synthetic records generated from copula fitted to care-intensity feature distributions
XGBoost discriminator trained to distinguish real from synthetic records; AUROC 0.633
Sepsis-3 requires SOFA score >= 2 plus infection proxy
Primary Phase 3 model; most conservative test of care-intensity hypothesis
Both used for falsification analysis; MIMIC-IV is primary confirmatory, eICU is exploratory replication
AI systems benchmarked on administrative labels may learn billing patterns rather than biology
Sepsis remains among the leading causes of ICU mortality
Discriminator AUROC 0.460 (near chance) and care-only AUC 0.694 suggest pronounced care-process leakage
